person.name: William de Lindsay Lord of Luffness

nameForms.fullText: William de Lindsay Lord of Luffness

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary: SIR WILLIAM DE LINDSAY, Lord of Lamberton, Sheriff of Berwickshire, witnessed a charter of King Alexander as William, son of Walter de Lindsay. He also held Molesworth and Caldecote of the earldom of Huntingdon. He was ambassador to England 1237, and party, as a baron, to the treaty between Alexander and Henry in 1244. He married, about 1220, Alicia, daughter of William de Lancaster, Lord of Kendal, by Agnes de Brus, co-heiress with Helewise, wife of Peter de Brus, of her brother William de Lancaster and died about 1247.

zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Lindsay Lord of Luffness於大約1202年出生在蘇格蘭Forfarshire可萊吉時，他的父親William De Lindsay22歲，母親Alicia De Lancaster9歲。他和Alice de Lancaster, of Windermere至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。他在英國蘇格蘭貝維其可爾被列為sheriff。他去世於大約1247年在蘇格蘭培爾可萊吉享年47歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lindsay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Lindsey in Lincolnshire, England. This is first found in the form Lindissi, apparently a derivative of the British name of Lincoln . To this was later added the Old English element ēg ‘island’, since the place was virtually cut off by the surrounding fenland. The surname was taken to Scotland at an early date and is the name of an important and powerful Scottish family.
Irish: adopted as an equivalent ofvarious Gaelic names (see   Lindsey  ).

variants.name: Linzy
meanings: Altered form of Scottish, Irish, or English   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  . Compare   Lenzy  .

variants.name: Linzey
meanings: English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .

variants.name: Lindsey
meanings: Irish: shortened and Anglicized form of various Gaelic surnames, as for example Ó Loingsigh (see Lynch 1), Mac Giolla Fhionntóg (see McClintock ), and Ó Fhloinn (see Flynn and Lynn ).

variants.name: Lincoln
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of Lincoln, so named from an original British name Lindo- ‘lake’ + Latin colonia ‘settlement, colony’. The place was an important administrative center during the Roman occupation of Britain and in the Middle Ages it was a center for the manufacture of cloth, including the famous ‘Lincoln green’.
African American: adoption of the surname in 1 above, in honor of Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the US, who abolished slavery.
History: Abraham Lincoln (1809–65), 16th president of the US, was the son of an illiterate laborer, descended from Samuel Lincoln, who had emigrated from England to MA in 1637.

variants.name: Linsey
meanings: Scottish, Irish, or English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .
Americanized form of German Linse or Swiss German Linsi, see Lins .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Lindsay Lord of Luffness was born about 1202, in Craigie, Forfarshire, Scotland, his father, William De Lindsay, was 22 and his mother, Alicia De Lancaster, was 9. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Alice de Lancaster, of Windermere. His occupation is listed as sheriff in Berwickshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. He died about 1247, in Craigie, Perthshire, Scotland, at the age of 47, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Lindsay Lord of Luffness est né environ 1202, à Craigie, Forfarshire, Écosse, son père, William De Lindsay, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Alicia De Lancaster, avait 9 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Alice de Lancaster, of Windermere. Sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff à Berwickshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé environ 1247, à Craigie, Perthshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Lindsay Lord of Luffness um 1202 in Craigie, Forfarshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater William De Lindsay 22 und seine Mutter Alicia De Lancaster 9. Er hatte mit Alice de Lancaster, of Windermere mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als sheriff in Berwickshire, Schottland, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb um 1247 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Craigie, Perthshire, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Alice de Lancaster, of Windermere. La sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff nel Berwickshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Morì circa 1247, nel Craigie, Perthshire, Scotland, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolto a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Lindsay Lord of Luffnessが約1202年にスコットランドForfarshireクレイジで生まれた時，父William De Lindsayは22歳，母Alicia De Lancasterは9歳であった。彼はAlice de Lancaster, of Windermereとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。イギリススコットランドバーウィクシュアイアでの職業はsheriffと記録されている。彼は約1247年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアクレイジで47歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Lindsay Lord of Luffness(이)가 대략 1202년 스컷랜드 Forfarshire 크레이기에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William De Lindsay(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Alicia De Lancaster(은)는 9세였다. 그는 Alice de Lancaster, of Windermere(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 스코틀랜드 버윅실르에서 sheriff이었다. 그는 대략 1247년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 크레이기에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas com Alice de Lancaster, of Windermere. Sua profissão aparece como sheriff em Berwickshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Lindsay Lord of Luffness родился около 1202 г. в местности креиги, Forfarshire, Шотландия, его отцу по имени William De Lindsay было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Alicia De Lancaster, 9. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери от Alice de Lancaster, of Windermere.  Он работал по профессии sheriff в местности бирвиккшир, Шотландия, Великобритания. Он умер около 1247 г. в местности креиги, пиртшир, Шотландия в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.
